description: Behavior checks an agent loaded with the simpod skill should pass. Each case lists the user prompt, the key actions/points a correct response must contain (rubric), and the SKILL.md section that grounds it. 'must_not' flags common wrong answers the skill is meant to prevent.

evals.prompt: I'm running an iPhone 16 Pro simulator. Tap the center of the screen, then send the app to the background.
rubric: Verifies a simulator is booted before issuing commands (e.g. xcrun simctl list devices booted, or simpod --list)
Taps with normalized coords: simpod tap 0.5 0.5
Backgrounds with simpod button home
Targets the device by name/UDID with -d when ambiguous
must_not: synthesizing the tap from gesture begin/end calls

evals.prompt: Show me my simulator right here — don't make me open another window.
rubric: Starts the server with simpod --detach -q and parses the JSON
Surfaces the url (http://127.0.0.1:5210) plainly as a manual fallback
Probes the session's tools for a preview/URL-opener (preview_start, mcp__Claude_Preview__preview_start, browser_open, ...) and hands off the url if present
If no such tool exists, says so and tells the user to open the URL themselves
must_not: inventing a host-specific preview tool that isn't in the session
evals.reference: SKILL.md (Show the stream in your agent's preview)

evals.prompt: There's a Submit button somewhere on screen. Tap it without me telling you where it is.
rubric: Reads the accessibility tree with simpod describe
Finds the node whose label/title matches 'Submit' (case-insensitive)
Takes the node frame center and normalizes it against the device screen size, then simpod tap <nx> <ny>
If no match is found, reports 'target not found' rather than guessing
must_not: tapping a guessed coordinate when the AX lookup returns nothing

evals.prompt: Switch the simulator to dark mode and rotate it to landscape (rotated left).
rubric: Sets appearance with simpod appearance dark
Rotates with simpod rotate landscape_left
Uses the documented orientation vocabulary (portrait | portrait_upside_down | landscape_left | landscape_right)

evals.prompt: Open the deep link myapp://checkout in the simulator and mock the GPS location to Apple Park (37.3349, -122.0090).
rubric: Opens the link with simpod open-url myapp://checkout
Mocks GPS with simpod location 37.3349 -122.0090 (latitude then longitude)
must_not: using xcrun simctl openurl when the skill exposes simpod open-url

evals.prompt: Make the status bar show 9:41 and 80% battery for a screenshot, then put it back to normal afterwards.
rubric: Overrides with simpod status-bar --battery 80 --time 9:41
Clears afterwards with simpod status-bar --clear

evals.prompt: I started a simpod stream earlier for a quick check and I'm done now. Clean it up.
rubric: Stops the detached server/helpers with simpod --kill (optionally -d <UDID> for one device)
Explains that a detached server is stopped with --kill, not Ctrl+C
must_not: telling the user to Ctrl+C a detached/background server

evals.prompt: Tap the login button on my simulator.
rubric: Checks prerequisites first: macOS, xcrun, and a booted simulator (xcrun simctl list devices booted)
If none is booted, boots one (simpod boots the first available, or xcrun simctl boot <UDID>) rather than failing silently
Prefers locating the button via simpod describe over guessing coordinates
must_not: assuming a simulator is booted without checking
